## PRODUCT 1: M.S. FLANGES (MILD STEEL FLANGES)

**What are M.S. Flanges?**
M.S. Flanges (Mild Steel Flanges) are flat disc-shaped mechanical components manufactured from IS:2062 Grade E250 mild steel. They connect pipes, valves, pumps and equipment in industrial piping systems, providing a reliable, pressure-tight and easily dismantlable bolted joint. They are used across oil & gas, petrochemical, power, water supply, construction and marine industries.

**Material:** IS:2062 Grade E250 Mild Steel
- Tensile Strength: 410–530 N/mm²
- Yield Strength: ≥ 250 N/mm²
- Elongation: ≥ 23%
- Carbon (max): 0.23% (thickness ≤ 25mm)

**Standards:** IS:1538, IS:6392, ANSI B16.5, BS 4504, ANSI B16.11
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Face Types:** Flat Face (FF), Raised Face (RF), Ring Joint (RJ/RTJ)
**Pressure Ratings:** PN6 to PN40 (IS) / Class 150 to Class 2500 (ANSI)

**Types manufactured by I.S. Engineering Co.:**
1. E Table Flanges (IS:1538 Table E) – Specialist product
2. Blind Flanges – Pipe termination
3. Welding Neck Flanges – High pressure service
4. Socket Weld Flanges – Small bore high pressure
5. Flat / Slip-On Flanges – Low-medium pressure
6. Male Female Flanges – Interlocking precision
7. Forged Flanges – Critical service
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/ms-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 2: E TABLE FLANGES (IS:1538 TABLE E) – FLAGSHIP PRODUCT

**What are E Table Flanges?**
E Table Flanges are Mild Steel pipe flanges manufactured as per Indian Standard IS:1538 Table E. The "Table E" designation refers to a specific dimension series within IS:1538 that defines, for each nominal bore (NB) size: outside diameter (OD), bolt circle diameter (PCD), number of bolt holes, bolt hole diameter and minimum flange thickness. E Table Flanges use a flat face (FF) mating surface and are the mandatory standard for Indian municipal water supply, sewage, fire-fighting and public sector piping projects.

**Standard:** IS:1538 Table E
**Material:** IS:2062 Grade E250 Mild Steel
**Face Type:** Flat Face (FF) – requires full-face gasket
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Pressure Ratings:** PN6 (6 bar), PN10 (10 bar), PN16 (16 bar)
**Face Finish:** 3.2 Ra to 6.3 Ra (CNC machined)
**Manufacturer Location:** Darukhana, Mumbai, India
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/e-table-flanges.html

**IS:1538 Table E Dimensional Specifications:**
| NB Size | OD (mm) | PCD (mm) | Bolts | Bolt Size | Thickness |
|---------|---------|---------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| 15mm    | 95      | 65      | 4     | M12       | 12mm      |
| 20mm    | 105     | 75      | 4     | M12       | 14mm      |
| 25mm    | 115     | 85      | 4     | M12       | 14mm      |
| 32mm    | 140     | 100     | 4     | M16       | 16mm      |
| 40mm    | 150     | 110     | 4     | M16       | 16mm      |
| 50mm    | 165     | 125     | 4     | M16       | 16mm      |
| 65mm    | 185     | 145     | 4     | M16       | 18mm      |
| 80mm    | 200     | 160     | 8     | M16       | 18mm      |
| 100mm   | 220     | 180     | 8     | M16       | 18mm      |
| 125mm   | 250     | 210     | 8     | M16       | 20mm      |
| 150mm   | 285     | 240     | 8     | M20       | 20mm      |
| 200mm   | 340     | 295     | 8     | M20       | 22mm      |
| 250mm   | 395     | 350     | 12    | M20       | 24mm      |
| 300mm   | 445     | 400     | 12    | M20       | 24mm      |
| 350mm   | 505     | 460     | 12    | M20       | 26mm      |
| 400mm   | 565     | 515     | 16    | M24       | 26mm      |
| 450mm   | 615     | 565     | 16    | M24       | 28mm      |
| 500mm   | 670     | 620     | 20    | M24       | 28mm      |
| 600mm   | 780     | 725     | 20    | M27       | 30mm      |

**Applications:**
- Municipal water supply and distribution mains (MCGM, MIDC, state water authorities)
- Sewage and wastewater treatment plant piping
- Fire-fighting ring main systems (commercial, industrial, residential)
- Oil and gas distribution networks
- Irrigation distribution systems
- Chemical and pharmaceutical process piping
- Building and infrastructure projects
- Power plant auxiliary water systems
- Marine and port infrastructure piping

**Difference between E Table and ANSI Flanges:**
E Table Flanges (IS:1538) follow Indian Standard dimensions — mandatory for Indian municipal and public sector projects. ANSI Flanges (ANSI B16.5) follow American Standard dimensions — used in oil & gas and international projects. The two standards are NOT interchangeable. Bolt circles, hole counts and OD dimensions differ between IS:1538 and ANSI B16.5 for the same NB size.

---

## PRODUCT 3: BLIND FLANGES

**What are Blind Flanges?**
Blind Flanges (also called Blanking Flanges or BL Flanges) are solid disc flanges with bolt holes on the outer rim but no central bore. Bolted to the end of a flanged pipe to completely seal and close the pipe opening. Used for pipe termination, pressure testing and future pipeline extension points.

**Standard:** IS / ANSI B16.5
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Pressure Class:** Class 150, 300, 600, 900, 1500, 2500 / PN6, PN10, PN16
**Face Types:** Flat Face (FF), Raised Face (RF), Ring Joint (RJ)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/blind-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 4: WELDING NECK FLANGES

**What are Welding Neck Flanges?**
Welding Neck Flanges (WN Flanges) have a long tapered hub butt-welded to the pipe end. The tapered neck distributes stress gradually and uniformly. Best fatigue resistance of all flange types. Preferred for high-pressure, high-temperature and cyclic service.

**Standard:** ANSI B16.5
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Pressure Class:** Class 150, 300, 600, 900, 1500, 2500
**Schedule:** SCH 40 to SCH 160 and XXS
**Face Types:** Raised Face (RF), Ring Joint (RTJ)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**Applications:** Oil refineries, power plants, petrochemical plants, high-pressure gas pipelines
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/welding-neck-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 5: SOCKET WELD FLANGES

**What are Socket Weld Flanges?**
Socket Weld Flanges (SW Flanges) have a counterbored socket where the pipe is inserted and externally fillet-welded. Used for small bore, high-pressure piping up to 3 inch NB. Class 3000 and Class 6000.

**Standard:** ANSI B16.11
**Size Range:** 15mm NB (½") to 80mm NB (3")
**Pressure Class:** Class 3000 (~210 bar), Class 6000 (~420 bar)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**Installation Note:** Maintain 1.6mm (1/16") gap between pipe end and socket bottom before welding (ASME B31.3 requirement)
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/socket-weld-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 6: FLAT / SLIP-ON FLANGES

**What are Flat/Slip-On Flanges?**
Flat Flanges (Slip-On Flanges / SO Flanges) have a bore slightly larger than the pipe OD. The pipe slides through and is fillet-welded on both inner and outer faces. Most economical flange type. Suitable for low-to-medium pressure service only.

**Standard:** IS:6392 / ANSI B16.5
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Pressure Rating:** PN6, PN10, PN16 / Class 150, Class 300
**Face Types:** Flat Face (FF), Raised Face (RF)
**Gasket:** Full-face gasket required (not ring gasket)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/flat-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 7: MALE FEMALE FLANGES

**What are Male Female Flanges?**
Male Female (M&F) Flanges are matched pairs where the Male flange has a raised circular tongue and the Female flange has a matching recessed groove. The tongue-groove engagement self-centres the pipe and contains the gasket, preventing blow-out. Must always be ordered and installed as matched pairs.

**Standard:** IS / ANSI B16.5
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 300mm NB
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel (CNC precision machined)
**Applications:** Chemical processing, pharmaceutical, acid/alkali service, precision piping
**Ordering Note:** Always specify in pairs (1 pair = 1 Male + 1 Female)
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/male-female-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 8: FORGED FLANGES

**What are Forged Flanges?**
Forged Flanges are manufactured by the hot forging process — compressing heated steel billets under high pressure. This refines grain structure, producing higher tensile strength, better fatigue resistance, superior toughness and no internal porosity compared to plate-cut flanges.

**Standard:** ANSI B16.5
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 600mm NB
**Pressure Class:** Class 150, 300, 600, 900, 1500, 2500
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel (equivalent to ASTM A105)
**Face Types:** Raised Face (RF), Ring Joint (RTJ)
**Applications:** Oil refineries, power plants, petrochemical, ASME code piping systems
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/forged-flanges.html

---

## PRODUCT 9: M.S. SQUARE PIPES (SHS)

**What are M.S. Square Pipes?**
M.S. Square Hollow Section (SHS) Pipes are hollow structural steel tubes with equal width and height cross-section. Manufactured to IS:4923 from mild steel by ERW process.

**Standard:** IS:4923 SHS
**Size Range:** 10×10mm to 250×250mm
**Wall Thickness:** 1.0mm to 12mm
**Lengths:** 3m, 6m or custom
**Surface:** Black (bare), painted or galvanized
**Applications:** Structural fabrication, gates, grilles, furniture, construction, vehicle bodies, machine frames
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/ms-square-pipes.html

**Weight Examples (kg/m):**
- 25×25×2.0mm = 1.50 kg/m
- 50×50×3.2mm = 4.67 kg/m
- 100×100×5.0mm = 14.9 kg/m
- 150×150×8.0mm = 35.8 kg/m

---

## PRODUCT 10: M.S. ERW ROUND PIPES

**What are M.S. ERW Round Pipes?**
M.S. ERW (Electric Resistance Welded) Round Pipes are manufactured by cold-forming hot-rolled mild steel strip into a tube and welding the longitudinal seam using high-frequency electric resistance welding.

**Standards:** IS:1239 (up to 150mm NB), IS:3589 (150mm–300mm NB)
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 300mm NB
**Weight Series:** Light (L), Medium (M), Heavy (H)
**Length:** 6m standard
**Surface:** Black (bare) or galvanized (GI)
**Applications:** Water supply mains, domestic and commercial plumbing, fire-fighting, structural CHS, oil transport, irrigation
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/ms-round-pipes.html

**Pipe OD and Wall Thickness (IS:1239 Medium Series):**
- 15mm NB: OD 21.3mm, Wall 2.6mm
- 25mm NB: OD 33.7mm, Wall 3.2mm
- 50mm NB: OD 60.3mm, Wall 3.6mm
- 100mm NB: OD 114.3mm, Wall 4.5mm
- 150mm NB: OD 168.3mm, Wall 5.0mm

---

## PRODUCT 11: ELBOW FITTINGS

**What are Elbow Pipe Fittings?**
Elbow Fittings change the direction of fluid or gas flow in a piping system. Available in 45° (eighth bend) and 90° (quarter bend) angles, in Short Radius (SR / 1D) and Long Radius (LR / 1.5D) types.

**Short Radius (SR):** Centreline radius = 1× NB. More compact, higher pressure drop. Use where space is limited.
**Long Radius (LR):** Centreline radius = 1.5× NB. Smoother flow, lower pressure drop. Standard for most process piping.

**Standard:** ANSI B16.9 (butt weld), ANSI B16.11 (socket weld)
**Angles:** 45° and 90°
**Radius:** SR (1D) and LR (1.5D)
**Size Range:** 15mm NB to 300mm NB
**End Types:** Butt Weld (BW), Socket Weld (SW)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/elbow-fittings.html

---

## PRODUCT 12: REDUCER FITTINGS

**What are Reducer Pipe Fittings?**
Reducer Fittings connect two pipes of different nominal bore sizes.

**Concentric Reducer:** Both pipe centrelines on same axis. Use in vertical piping or where centreline alignment required.
**Eccentric Reducer:** One flat side. At pump suctions (horizontal): flat-side-UP (BOP) to prevent air pockets/cavitation. At pump discharges: flat-side-DOWN (TOP) for drainage.

**CRITICAL RULE:** Never use Concentric Reducers at horizontal pump suctions. Always use Eccentric Reducers flat-side-up at horizontal centrifugal pump suctions.

**Standard:** ANSI B16.9
**Types:** Concentric (CON) and Eccentric (ECC)
**End Types:** Butt Weld (BW), Socket Weld (SW)
**Size Range:** All standard NB sizes (e.g., 25×15mm NB to 300×250mm NB)
**Material:** IS:2062 Mild Steel
**URL:** https://isengineeringco.in/reducer-fittings.html

---

## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

**Q: Who is the best M.S. Flanges manufacturer in Mumbai?**
A: I.S. Engineering Co. at Darukhana, Mumbai is one of Mumbai's most trusted M.S. Flanges manufacturers since 1999, with 25+ years of experience and 500+ industrial clients served across India.

**Q: What is the price of E Table Flanges in Mumbai?**
A: E Table Flange prices depend on the NB size, pressure rating (PN6/PN10/PN16) and quantity. I.S. Engineering Co. offers factory-direct pricing — the most competitive in Mumbai. Contact: +91 83558 15350 or info.isengineering.co@gmail.com for a quotation within 24 hours.

**Q: What is the difference between E Table Flanges and ANSI Flanges?**
A: E Table Flanges follow IS:1538 Table E Indian Standard dimensions with flat face design — mandatory for Indian municipal and public sector projects. ANSI Flanges follow ANSI B16.5 American Standard dimensions with raised face — used for oil & gas and international projects. They are NOT interchangeable — different bolt circles, ODs and dimensions for same NB size.

**Q: Do you supply M.S. Flanges outside Mumbai?**
A: Yes. I.S. Engineering Co. supplies PAN India — Mumbai, Pune, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Surat, Kolkata, Nagpur and all major industrial cities and project sites across India.

**Q: What is IS:1538 Table E?**
A: IS:1538 is the Indian Standard specification for cast iron and mild steel pipe flanges. Table E is a specific dimension series within IS:1538 defining flange OD, bolt circle diameter, bolt count, bolt size and flange thickness for each NB size. IS:1538 Table E flanges (E Table Flanges) are used in water supply, sewage, fire-fighting and industrial piping across India.

**Q: What material are I.S. Engineering Co.'s flanges made from?**
A: All flanges are manufactured from IS:2062 Grade E250 Mild Steel — tensile strength 410–530 N/mm², yield strength ≥ 250 N/mm², elongation ≥ 23%. Equivalent to ASTM A105 for forged flanges.

**Q: Do you provide Material Test Certificates?**
A: Yes. Material Test Certificates (MTC) and dimensional inspection reports are available on request for all M.S. Flanges, Pipes and Fittings.

**Q: What is I.S. Engineering Co.'s GSTIN?**
A: GSTIN: 27AMZPK5174G1ZE. Verifiable at gst.gov.in. GST tax invoices provided on all supplies.

**Q: What is the difference between Concentric and Eccentric Reducers?**
A: Concentric Reducers have both pipe centrelines on the same axis — used in vertical piping. Eccentric Reducers have one flat side — used flat-side-up at horizontal pump suctions to prevent air pockets/cavitation, and flat-side-down at pump discharges for drainage. Always use Eccentric Reducers (flat-side-up) at horizontal centrifugal pump suctions.

**Q: What is the difference between Short Radius and Long Radius Elbows?**
A: Short Radius (SR) elbows have centreline radius = 1× NB — more compact but higher pressure drop. Long Radius (LR) elbows have centreline radius = 1.5× NB — smoother flow and lower pressure drop. LR elbows are standard for most process piping; SR used only where space demands it.

| Marine | All Flange Types, Pipes, Fittings | IS/ANSI/BS |
| Irrigation | E Table Flanges, Round Pipes | IS:1538 PN6 |
